differentials.name: reduced range of motion; pain ← increase in intramedullary pressure ← medullary bone marrow edema Location:   femoral head (most common), humeral head, femoral condyles, proximal tibia, distal femoral metadiaphysis, distal tibial metadiaphysis, scaphoid, lunate, talus

differentials.raw: Fibrosarcoma (most common), malignant fibrous histiocytoma, benign cysts

differentials.raw: hip / groin / thigh / knee pain; limited range of motion

differentials.raw: core decompression (for grade 0-II): most successful with < 25% involvement of femoral head

differentials.name: arthroplasty / arthrodesis / total hip replacement
differentials.raw: arthroplasty / arthrodesis / total hip replacement (for grade > III)

differentials.name: Transient osteoporosis of the hip = bone marrow edema syndrome
differentials.raw: Transient osteoporosis of the hip = bone marrow edema syndrome (marked diffuse increased SI on long repetition time images + diffuse contrast enhancement, no reactive interface)

differentials.name: Subchondral epiphyseal insufficiency fracture
differentials.raw: Subchondral epiphyseal insufficiency fracture (low SI band in superolateral femoral head convex toward articular surface; speckled / linear hypointense areas, focal depression of epiphyseal contour)

context: Sickle cell disease, Spontaneous osteonecrosis of knee Path: (a)  Stage of cell death: cellular ischemia / anoxia → death of hematopoietic cells (in 6-12 hours) > adipocytes > bone cells = osteoclasts and osteoblasts + osteocytes (in 12-48 hours) ◊  Chondrocytes are adapted to relatively low oxygen tension and do not become devitalized! (b)  Stage of ↑ vascularity / reparative phase (osteoclasis): trabecular resorption ← inflammatory fibrovascular infiltration + proliferation ← hyperemia mixed with areas of relatively increased trabecular density ← osteonecrosis (c)  Stage of substitution / re
